facebook.caption: Sitting at 4.7 out of 5 stars across 263 reviews, Vinpearl Phú Quốc consistently earns its reputation as one of the island's most beloved resort stays. Nestled along Bãi Dài in the quieter Gành Dầu area of northern Phu Quoc, it offers the kind of distance from the crowds that makes a holiday feel like a real escape. Expect spacious grounds, a long stretch of relatively uncrowded beach, and the full resort experience. Practical tip: Bãi Dài is about 30 km from Phu Quoc town, so plan ahead if you want to explore local markets or street food.
facebook.video_caption: Watch those wide open grounds unfold, the beach at Bãi Dài looking impossibly calm compared to the busier southern shores. This is what a proper resort retreat in Phu Quoc looks like. Fewer crowds, more space, and that northern coastline doing all the heavy lifting.

instagram.caption: Northern Phu Quoc has a different energy, quieter, more spacious, and Vinpearl Phú Quốc on Bãi Dài captures that perfectly. Rated 4.7/5 by 263 guests, this resort is the move if you want beach without the bustle.
instagram.video_caption: That long, calm stretch of Bãi Dài with almost no one on it. Vinpearl Phú Quốc is giving full resort vibes in the quietest corner of the island.

tiktok.caption: The quieter side of Phu Quoc hits different. Vinpearl Phú Quốc on Bãi Dài is your sign to skip the crowds. 4.7 stars, 263 reviews. #VinpearlPhuQuoc #PhuQuocResort #VietnamTravel #BaiDai

30 km from town, plan your day trips

facebook.caption: Not every great coffee shop announces itself loudly, and Tiệm Cà Phê Đồng Giao on 38 Hoàng Văn Thụ is proof of that. Tucked into the local side of Phu Quoc, this small café holds a near-perfect 4.9/5 rating from its guests, which tells you everything you need to know. Come for a slow Vietnamese coffee, soak in the neighborhood atmosphere, and take a break from the beach crowds. Practical tip: it sits in a quieter residential stretch, so grab a seat, order slowly, and enjoy the pace.
facebook.video_caption: Watch the coffee hit the glass, the condensation form, and the whole café slow down around it. Tiệm Cà Phê Đồng Giao is one of those places you stumble into and end up staying far longer than planned. A 4.9-star local gem in Phu Quoc that earns every single one.

instagram.caption: Local vibes, near-perfect ratings, and a really good cup of Vietnamese coffee. Tiệm Cà Phê Đồng Giao sits at 38 Hoàng Văn Thụ and is exactly the kind of place Phu Quoc does not advertise enough. Go find it.

items.placeName: AROMA Cafe Bistro Bar Phu Quoc

facebook.caption: Not every great coffee spot in Phu Quoc sits on the beach. AROMA Cafe Bistro Bar Phu Quoc, tucked inside Happy Bear Hostel on 179 30/4 Street, has quietly earned a 4.9 out of 5 from nearly 450 reviewers, and that kind of rating does not happen by accident. Whether you are grabbing a morning brew before hitting the beaches or winding down with something cold in the afternoon, this cozy bistro-bar hybrid hits the spot. Practical tip: it is inside a hostel, so the vibe is social and relaxed. Walk in, say hello, and do not be surprised if you end up staying much longer than planned.
facebook.video_caption: Watch the steam rise, hear the quiet buzz of conversation, and suddenly a hostel cafe feels like the best seat in Phu Quoc. AROMA Cafe Bistro Bar Phu Quoc on 30/4 Street is the kind of place the clips speak for themselves. Nearly 450 people gave it a 4.9 out of 5. Come see why.

instagram.caption: A 4.9-star cafe hiding inside a hostel? AROMA Cafe Bistro Bar Phu Quoc on 30/4 Street is exactly that kind of surprise. Chill atmosphere, great drinks, and nearly 450 reviewers who clearly agree. Add it to your Phu Quoc list.
instagram.video_caption: Good coffee, easy vibes, and a 4.9 rating from real travelers. AROMA Cafe Bistro Bar Phu Quoc might be the most underrated stop on the island. Walk in, order something, stay a while.

facebook.caption: Tarapaca Cafe on Đường Trần Hưng Đạo is the kind of spot that earns 4.7 stars across 723 reviews for very good reason. Whether you need a proper espresso to start your Phu Quoc morning or a cool drink to recover from a long afternoon on the beach, this cafe delivers consistency that is surprisingly rare on the island. It sits right on one of the main strips, making it an easy stop between sightseeing and dinner. Practical tip: visit in the morning or late afternoon to snag a good seat before the evening crowd rolls in.
facebook.video_caption: Watch the steam rise, the drinks land on the table, and the whole vibe of Tarapaca Cafe come to life. This is what a 4.7-star coffee stop on Đường Trần Hưng Đạo actually looks like, and yes, it lives up to the reviews. If your Phu Quoc itinerary needs a reliable caffeine anchor, you just found it.

facebook.caption: HD cafe in Khu 1 is one of those low-key local spots that foreign visitors often walk straight past, and that's exactly why you should stop in. Rated 4.5 out of 5 from over 300 reviews, this little coffee shop on 163 8, Khu 1, Phú Quốc has clearly earned its loyal crowd. Expect Vietnamese-style coffee, a relaxed pace, and a genuine neighborhood feel far from the tourist bustle. Tip: order a cà phê sữa đá and find a seat before the morning rush fills the place up.
facebook.video_caption: Watch the slow drip of Vietnamese iced coffee hit the glass, condensation already forming before you even take a seat. HD cafe keeps things simple and unhurried, and the 300-plus regulars who gave it a 4.5-star rating clearly appreciate every sip. This is your morning sorted.

instagram.caption: Skip the resort breakfast and start your morning at HD cafe in Khu 1. Over 300 locals and travelers have rated it 4.5 stars, and one sip of the iced coffee will tell you exactly why. Unhurried, authentic, and very easy to love.

items.placeName: MR. VIET COFFEE OUTLET IN PHU QUOC

facebook.caption: A perfect 5-star rating with 97 reviews is hard to argue with, and Mr. Viet Coffee at Kiot 20, 22 Đ. Lê Lợi in Phú Quốc is clearly doing something right. If you are searching for a genuine Vietnamese coffee experience on the island, this little spot is worth tracking down. Grab a cà phê sữa đá, slow down, and watch island life go by. Practical tip: the address sits in a kiot-style shophouse row, so look for the signage carefully and you will find it.
facebook.video_caption: Watch that iced coffee come together, layer by layer, the Vietnamese way. Mr. Viet Coffee on Đ. facebook.caption: Hoa Đảo Viên Coffee - Phú Quốc is the kind of spot that sneaks up on you. Tucked away on the island, this highly rated café (4.6 stars from 213 reviews) has quietly won over locals and travelers alike. Expect a relaxed, garden-style atmosphere perfect for slowing down with a Vietnamese iced coffee in hand. facebook.caption: Hồ Bơi Cafe Nam Anh is turning heads on the island with a near-perfect 4.8 out of 5 stars from 30 reviews, and it is easy to see why locals and visitors keep coming back. This Vietnamese restaurant in Phu Quoc offers the kind of honest, home-style cooking that puts a lot of tourist-trap spots to shame. If you are looking to eat the way the island actually eats, this is a solid stop to add to your list. Tip: with only 30 reviews so far, it is still a hidden gem, so arrive early to beat any wait.
facebook.video_caption: Watch the food land on the table at Hồ Bơi Cafe Nam Anh and you will understand why the rating sits at 4.8. Fresh, unfussy Vietnamese plates served up in a relaxed setting that feels nothing like the crowded tourist strips. This is Phu Quoc eating on its own terms, and it looks every bit as good as it tastes.

instagram.caption: 4.8 stars and still flying under the radar. Hồ Bơi Cafe Nam Anh is the kind of Vietnamese restaurant that rewards curious travelers who wander off the main drag. Real food, real flavors, real Phu Quoc. Go early and thank yourself later.

facebook.caption: Tucked inside Sunset Town, Lotus Home & Cafe at 69 Positano is the kind of place that earns its 4.8-star rating one cup at a time. The name says it all: it feels genuinely home-like, with a cafe personality that fits right into Phu Quoc's relaxed pace. Over 1,100 visitors have left happy, and it's easy to see why this spot keeps drawing people back. Practical tip: Sunset Town itself is gorgeous in the late afternoon, so pair your visit with a stroll along the waterfront before or after your coffee.
facebook.video_caption: Watch the light fall over Sunset Town and suddenly every corner feels like a postcard. This walkthrough of Lotus Home & Cafe shows you exactly the kind of cozy, welcoming atmosphere that keeps its review score sitting at a very impressive 4.8 out of 5. Find it at 69 Positano and let the neighborhood do the rest.

instagram.caption: A 4.8-star cafe inside Phu Quoc's dreamy Sunset Town district. Lotus Home & Cafe at 69 Positano has over 1,100 happy visitors singing its praises, and the home-like atmosphere makes it easy to linger. Time your visit for late afternoon.
